title: Biomass trajectories (cpa=1/1e6/1e9) vs Beulig 2025 batch endpoint
caption: log-y population.biomass_concentration_gL(t) for cpa ∈ {1, 1e6, 1e9} v2ecoli runs alongside Beulig 2025's batch-phase endpoint band at t=6h. Time-series counterpart to chart 00.
interpretation: Visual rendering of the 4-5 order density-gap at the same time-axis as Beulig's batch endpoint. v2ecoli's 60-min trajectories end far below Beulig's 6-h endpoint band, and the slopes don't suggest the gap would close with a 6x duration extension — per-cell mass plateaus after ~1 generation in the M9-glucose calibration regime. Closing the gap needs the architectural moves listed in chart 00's meta (longer duration via fed-batch / impossibly-large cpa / candidate surrogate engine in mbp-06).
